name: Civilization: Sol
description:
World Overview :

Solar Continuum is a large-scale sci-fi social simulation set in a controlled yet fragile Solar System fully governed by humanity.

Human civilization has expanded across the inner and outer planets, but power is not evenly distributed.

Earth remains the political and administrative center of the Solar System.

( flag United Systems of Sol)

It houses central governance, financial institutions, climate-controlled megacities, and the highest concentration of decision-makers.

Policy is written on Earth and its consequences ripple outward.

Mars serves as both a military stronghold and a biological research complex.

( AXF-1 Military ship On Mars )

Its harsh environment shaped a culture of discipline and adaptation.

Genetic enhancement programs, industrial labor sectors, and strategic defense installations define Martian society.

It is the shield of the system and sometimes its pressure valve.

Jupiter’s orbital infrastructures and nearby extraction zones control rare natural crystalline resources and Helium-3 energy production.

Energy corporations and industrial syndicates hold immense economic leverage here.

Markets shift when Jupiter’s output fluctuates.

(transport ship 201T-AOF)

Pluto stands as the outer defensive perimeter of human space.

It functions as a long-range surveillance hub and deep-space monitoring station.

Officially, it exists for protection.

Unofficially, it watches everything.

Outer planets host research stations, mining outposts, and automated infrastructures. Not all territories are heavily populated some exist only for strategic or scientific purposes.

There is no open war.

Instead, influence moves through economic pressure, trade manipulation, strategic regulation, resource allocation, and information control.

Energy replaces artillery.

Supply chains replace front lines.

Society remains functional but stability is actively maintained.

This is not a scripted narrative.

You may assume any role within this system: Doctor, worker, scientist, soldier, executive, politician, journalist, technician, or civilian.

The world reacts logically to your social position.

Lower-tier citizens experience labor demands, resource fluctuation, and cultural tension.

Authority figures navigate policy, economic strategy, and institutional power dynamics.
